info.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The DB instance identifier, unique for each DB instance

params.name: DBInstanceClass
params.description: The compute and memory capacity of the DB instance (e.g., db.m5.large)

params.name: Engine
params.description: The database engine to use (mysql, postgres, mariadb, oracle-ee, sqlserver-ee, aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)

params.name: MasterUsername
params.description: The name for the master user

params.name: MasterUserPassword
params.description: The password for the master user

params.name: AllocatedStorage
params.description: The amount of storage in gibibytes (GiB) to allocate for the DB instance

params.name: EngineVersion
params.description: The version number of the database engine to use

params.name: DBName
params.description: The name of the database to create when the DB instance is created

params.name: Port
params.description: The port number on which the database accepts connections

params.name: MultiAZ
params.description: Whether the DB instance is a Multi-AZ deployment

params.name: StorageType
params.description: The storage type to associate with the DB instance (gp2, gp3, io1, standard)

params.name: PubliclyAccessible
params.description: Whether the DB instance is publicly accessible

params.name: VpcSecurityGroupIds.member.1
params.description: A VPC security group to associate with the DB instance

params.name: DBSubnetGroupName
params.description: A DB subnet group to associate with this DB instance

items.docs: Creates a new DB instance. The new DB instance can be an RDS DB instance, or it can be a DB instance in an Aurora DB cluster. You can specify the DB engine, instance class, allocated storage, master username and password, and other configuration settings.

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB instance to describe

params.name: Filters.member.1.Name
params.description: The name of a filter

params.name: Filters.member.1.Values.member.1
params.description: A value for the filter

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response

params.name: Marker
params.description: An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

items.docs: Describes provisioned RDS instances. This API supports pagination. Returns information about the DB instances including engine, instance class, storage, endpoint, status, and configuration details.

info.name: Amazon RDS Modify a DB instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The DB instance identifier for the instance to be modified

params.name: DBInstanceClass
params.description: The new compute and memory capacity of the DB instance

params.name: AllocatedStorage
params.description: The new amount of storage in GiB to allocate for the DB instance

params.name: MasterUserPassword
params.description: The new password for the master user

params.name: MultiAZ
params.description: Whether the DB instance is a Multi-AZ deployment

params.name: EngineVersion
params.description: The version number of the database engine to upgrade to

params.name: ApplyImmediately
params.description: Whether to apply the modification immediately or during the next maintenance window

params.name: BackupRetentionPeriod
params.description: The number of days to retain automated backups (0-35)

params.name: StorageType
params.description: The storage type to associate with the DB instance

items.docs: Modifies settings for a DB instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request. Some modifications are applied immediately, while others are applied during the next maintenance window.

info.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The DB instance identifier for the DB instance to be deleted

params.name: SkipFinalSnapshot
params.description: Whether to skip the creation of a final DB snapshot before deleting the instance

params.name: FinalDBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the final DB snapshot to create before deleting the instance

params.name: DeleteAutomatedBackups
params.description: Whether to remove automated backups immediately after deletion

items.docs: Deletes a previously provisioned DB instance. When you delete a DB instance, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual DB snapshots are not deleted.

info.name: Amazon RDS Reboot a DB instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The DB instance identifier

params.name: ForceFailover
params.description: Whether to force a failover from one AZ to another in a Multi-AZ deployment

items.docs: Reboots a DB instance. The reboot causes a momentary outage and the database instance status is set to rebooting. A DB instance can be rebooted when it is in the available state. If the DB instance is in a Multi-AZ deployment, you can force a failover from one AZ to another.

info.name: Amazon RDS Create a DB snapshot

params.name: DBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier for the DB snapshot

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB instance to create the snapshot from

items.docs: Creates a snapshot of a DB instance. The source DB instance must be in the available or storage-optimization state. The DB snapshot is created with a snapshot type of manual.

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The ID of the DB instance to retrieve snapshots for

params.name: DBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: A specific DB snapshot identifier to describe

params.name: SnapshotType
params.description: The type of snapshots to return (automated, manual, shared, public, awsbackup)

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response

params.name: Marker
params.description: An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

items.docs: Returns information about DB snapshots. This API supports pagination. You can filter by DB instance identifier, DB snapshot identifier, snapshot type, and other criteria.

info.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB snapshot

params.name: DBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB snapshot to delete

items.docs: Deletes a DB snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated. The DB snapshot must be in the available state to be deleted.

info.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The DB cluster identifier, unique for each DB cluster

params.name: Engine
params.description: The database engine to use (aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)

params.name: EngineVersion
params.description: The version number of the database engine to use

params.name: MasterUsername
params.description: The name of the master user for the DB cluster

params.name: MasterUserPassword
params.description: The password for the master database user

params.name: DatabaseName
params.description: The name for a database to create in the DB cluster

params.name: Port
params.description: The port number on which the instances in the DB cluster accept connections

params.name: DBSubnetGroupName
params.description: A DB subnet group to associate with this DB cluster

params.name: VpcSecurityGroupIds.member.1
params.description: A VPC security group to associate with the DB cluster

params.name: StorageEncrypted
params.description: Whether the DB cluster is encrypted

params.name: BackupRetentionPeriod
params.description: The number of days for which automated backups are retained (1-35)

items.docs: Creates a new Amazon Aurora DB cluster or Multi-AZ DB cluster. You can use the ReplicationSourceIdentifier parameter to create an Amazon Aurora DB cluster as a read replica of another DB cluster or Amazon RDS MySQL or PostgreSQL DB instance.

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe

params.name: Filters.member.1.Name
params.description: The name of a filter

params.name: Filters.member.1.Values.member.1
params.description: A value for the filter

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response

params.name: Marker
params.description: An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

items.docs: Returns information about Amazon Aurora DB clusters and Multi-AZ DB clusters. This API supports pagination. For more information on Amazon Aurora DB clusters, see the Amazon Aurora User Guide.

info.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The DB cluster identifier for the DB cluster to be deleted

params.name: SkipFinalSnapshot
params.description: Whether to skip the creation of a final DB cluster snapshot before deleting the cluster

params.name: FinalDBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the final DB cluster snapshot to create before deleting the cluster

items.docs: Deletes a previously provisioned DB cluster. When you delete a DB cluster, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual DB cluster snapshots are not deleted.

info.name: Amazon RDS Describe DB engine versions

params.name: Engine
params.description: The database engine to return (mysql, postgres, mariadb, oracle-ee, sqlserver-ee, aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)

params.name: EngineVersion
params.description: The database engine version to return

params.name: DBParameterGroupFamily
params.description: The name of a specific DB parameter group family to return details for

params.name: DefaultOnly
params.description: Whether to return only the default version of the engine

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response

params.name: Marker
params.description: An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

items.docs: Returns a list of the available DB engines and their versions. You can filter by engine, engine version, DB parameter group family, and other criteria.
